inclusions: Coal (anthracite, bituminous, other), whether or not pulverised but not agglomerated; briquettes, ovoids and similar solid fuels manufactured from coal
Lignite (brown coal), whether or not agglomerated, excluding jet
Peat (including peat litter), whether or not agglomerated
Coke and semi-coke of coal, of lignite or of peat, whether or not agglomerated; retort carbon
Coal gas, water gas, producer gas and similar gases — other than petroleum gases and other gaseous hydrocarbons
Tar distilled from coal, from lignite or from peat, and other mineral tars, whether or not dehydrated or partially distilled, including reconstituted tars

Oils and other products of distillation of high temperature coal tar; similar products in which the weight of AROMATIC constituents EXCEEDS that of NON-AROMATIC constituents (benzene, toluene, xylenes, naphthalene, creosote oils, carbon black feedstock)
Pitch and pitch coke obtained from coal tar or from other mineral tars
Petroleum oils and oils obtained from bituminous minerals — CRUDE (including crude petroleum, condensates)

Petroleum oils and oils obtained from bituminous minerals — NON-CRUDE; preparations NESOI containing ≥70% by weight of petroleum oils; waste oils (light oils/motor spirit/diesel/jet fuel/lubricating oils/kerosene; biodiesel-containing preparations; waste oils including PCB/PCT/PBB waste)
Petroleum gases and other gaseous hydrocarbons — liquefied (LNG, propane, butane, ethylene, etc.) or in gaseous state (CNG, other)
Petroleum jelly; paraffin wax; microcrystalline wax; slack wax; ozokerite; lignite wax; peat wax; other mineral waxes; similar synthetic products
Petroleum coke (calcined and not calcined); petroleum bitumen; other residues of petroleum oils
Bitumen and asphalt, natural; bituminous or oil shale and tar sands; asphaltites and asphaltic rocks
Bituminous mixtures based on natural asphalt, natural bitumen, petroleum bitumen, mineral tar or mineral tar pitch (mastics, cut-backs)

exclusions.condition: Separate chemically defined organic compounds — EXCEPT pure methane and pure propane which go to heading 27.11
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 29 (organic chemicals) or appropriate chapter. Only pure methane and propane → 27.11

exclusions.condition: Mixed unsaturated hydrocarbons of heading 33.01, 33.02 or 38.05
Liquid synthetic polyolefins of which less than 60% by volume distils at 300°C (at 1,013 millibars reduced-pressure)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 39 (plastics) — these are NOT petroleum oils under heading 27.10 even if they resemble petroleum oils

classification_rules.rule: Note 1 — Only pure methane and propane among chemically defined compounds belong in Ch.27
classification_rules.description: Chapter 27 excludes separate chemically defined organic compounds. The only two exceptions are PURE methane and PURE propane → heading 27.11. All other chemically pure organic compounds → Chapter 29 or other relevant chapters.

classification_rules.rule: Anthracite definition (Subheading Note 1) — volatile matter threshold
classification_rules.description: Anthracite (subheading 2701.11) = coal with volatile matter limit NOT EXCEEDING 14% on a DRY, MINERAL-MATTER-FREE basis. Bituminous coal (2701.12) = volatile matter EXCEEDING 14% + calorific value ≥5,833 kcal/kg (moist, mineral-matter-free basis). Other coal = 2701.19.

CRUDE petroleum oils (including condensates) → heading 27.09. Non-crude petroleum oils (refined, processed) and preparations containing ≥70% petroleum oils → heading 27.10. The crude/non-crude distinction is the primary split between these two major headings.

Light oils and preparations (subheading 2710.12) = petroleum oil preparations of which 90% or more by volume (including losses) distil at 210°C according to ISO 3405 (equivalent to ASTM D 86 method). Motor spirit (gasoline), aviation spirit, white spirit, solvents, naphtha → typically qualify as light oils.

classification_rules.rule: Motor spirit (gasoline) sub-classification by RON and lead content
classification_rules.description: Motor spirit within 2710.12: First split by LEADED vs UNLEADED. Then by RON: ≥97, 90–97, other RON. For unleaded: further split by blending — unblended, blended with ethanol, other. Aviation spirit separately coded by octane rating (≥100 and other).

Petroleum oils (non-crude) or preparations ≥70% petroleum oils that CONTAIN BIODIESEL → subheading 2710.20 (separate from 2710.12 and 2710.19). Biodiesel = mono-alkyl esters of fatty acids used as fuel, derived from animal, vegetable or microbial fats/oils (per Subheading Note 5). Pure biodiesel (no petroleum) → heading 38.26.

classification_rules.rule: Note 2 — Extended scope of petroleum oils in heading 27.10
classification_rules.description: Heading 27.10 'petroleum oils' includes: (1) actual petroleum oils, (2) similar oils, and (3) oils consisting mainly of MIXED UNSATURATED HYDROCARBONS obtained by ANY process — PROVIDED the weight of NON-AROMATIC constituents EXCEEDS that of AROMATIC constituents. Exception: liquid synthetic polyolefins <60% vol distilling at 300°C → Chapter 39.

classification_rules.rule: Coal tar distillation products — aromatic ratio determines 27.07 vs. 27.10
classification_rules.description: Products of high-temperature coal tar distillation where AROMATIC > NON-AROMATIC by weight → heading 27.07 (benzene, toluene, xylene, naphthalene must each exceed 50% by weight per Subheading Note 3 for 2707.10/20/30/40). Products where NON-AROMATIC > AROMATIC → heading 27.10. This aromatic ratio is the key boundary.

Waste oils (2710.91/99) = waste containing MAINLY petroleum oils ± water, including: (a) used lubricating/hydraulic/transformer oils no longer fit as primary products; (b) sludge oils from petroleum storage tanks (mainly oil + high concentrations of additives); (c) oils as emulsions in water or mixed with water (oil spills, tank washings, cutting oils waste).
